Cross-Platform Engineer Text Editor

Avalonia implementation specification for coding agent handoff

Target platforms: Windows and Linux desktop

1. Product Summary

Build a lightweight cross-platform text editor for engineers using Avalonia. The editor focuses on fast multi-file editing, crash-resilient temporary recovery, engineer-friendly syntax support, formatting tools, Markdown preview, and predictable tab behavior.

  • Primary users: engineers editing configuration files, logs, scripts, notes, XML, JSON, YAML, and Markdown.

  • Primary UI direction: clean tabbed editor with no file explorer/sidebar and no compact-tab variant.

  • Core promise: never lose unsaved work across app restarts, normal closes, or crashes.

2. Approved Design Direction

Use the following high-resolution mockups as the visual baseline. Keep the general layout, tab treatment, status bar, line number gutter, and light/dark theme variations. Do not implement the earlier file-explorer/sidebar concept or compact-tab concept.

Figure 1. Approved UI direction: tabbed editor without file explorer and without compact tabs.

Design rule

Requirement

No file explorer

Do not include a left project tree, folder panel, or permanent file explorer pane.

No compact tabs

Use the regular tab design from the approved mockups.

Menu bar

Provide File, Edit, View, Search, Format, Settings, and Help menus.

Editor area

Primary content area is the code/text editor with optional line numbers and optional word wrap.

Status bar

Show cursor position, indentation mode/size, encoding, line ending, syntax mode, and save/recovery status.



3. Functional Requirements

ID

Requirement

Acceptance criteria

FR-01

Multiple open files with tabbed interface.

User can open, create, edit, switch, and close multiple tabs without data loss.

FR-02

New untitled tabs use Unfile-N naming.

First new tab is Unfile-1; subsequent tabs increment monotonically for the session: Unfile-2, Unfile-3, etc. Do not reuse a number until app restart unless the persisted recovery state requires it.

FR-03

Disk-backed files show file name only in the tab.

Opening C:\src\appsettings.json or /home/user/appsettings.json displays appsettings.json as the tab title.

FR-04

Each tab has a close X.

Clicking X initiates close flow for that document; unsaved changes are handled before closing.

FR-05

Unsaved changes indicator.

Tabs with unsaved changes display a visible indicator such as the approved red dot. Saved tabs show no red dot.

FR-06

Line numbers.

Editor can display line numbers in a gutter. Line numbers are toggleable.

FR-07

Word wrap.

Word wrap is toggleable per editor view and persists per user preference.

FR-08

Tab and Shift+Tab indent using 3 spaces.

Pressing Tab inserts or indents selected lines by exactly 3 spaces. Shift+Tab unindents by up to 3 leading spaces per selected line. No tab character should be inserted by default.

FR-09

Format menu for XML and JSON.

Format menu includes Format JSON and Format XML. Formatting should pretty-print valid documents and show a clear error for invalid documents without changing content.

FR-10

Syntax support for YAML, JSON, and XML.

Syntax mode is detected from extension and can be manually selected. Required extensions: .yaml, .yml, .json, .xml.

FR-11

Auto indentation on newline.

Pressing Enter preserves indentation from current line and optionally increases indent after known opening tokens such as {, [, <tag>, or YAML nesting patterns.

FR-12

Markdown preview in split panel.

Markdown files can show rendered preview beside the editor. Preview can be toggled on/off and updates as content changes.

FR-13

Search and replace.

Support in-document find, find next/previous, replace current, replace all, match case, whole word, and regex toggle.

FR-14

Temporary storage recovery.

All open tabs and unsaved contents are persisted to temporary storage frequently so work is recoverable after app close or crash.



4. Tab Behavior Specification

State

Tab title

Indicator

Close behavior

New unsaved document

Unfile-N

Unsaved red dot immediately after first edit. Optional: new blank tab may start clean until edited.

If unchanged, close immediately. If changed, ask Save / Discard / Cancel.

Opened file, clean

File name only, e.g., settings.json

No unsaved indicator.

Close immediately.

Opened file, dirty

File name only, e.g., settings.json

Unsaved red dot.

Ask Save / Discard / Cancel.

Recovered unsaved document

Recovered: Unfile-N or original file name

Unsaved red dot until saved.

Ask Save / Discard / Cancel.



  • Clicking a tab activates that document and updates the editor text, status bar, syntax mode, and Markdown preview state.

  • Clicking the tab X must not accidentally close the wrong document if the active tab changes during the click event.

  • Middle-click close is optional, but if implemented it must follow the same unsaved-change flow.

  • A plus button for new tab is allowed and should create the next Unfile-N document.

5. Temporary Storage and Crash Recovery

The app must persist all open tabs to temporary storage so work survives normal closing, app restart, and crashes. Treat temp persistence as an autosave journal, not as a replacement for the user explicitly saving files.

Item

Specification

Storage location

Use an OS-appropriate app data/temp location. The implementation should use Avalonia/NET APIs to resolve a per-user app directory, e.g., LocalApplicationData or a platform-equivalent location.

State file

Persist an editor-session manifest such as session.json with open tab order, active tab id, file path, display title, syntax mode, word wrap setting, line-number setting, dirty flag, cursor position, scroll position, and recovery blob reference.

Content blobs

Persist each tab content to a separate recovery blob keyed by stable tab id. Do not store all unsaved content only in one large file.

Write cadence

Persist on content change using a short debounce, on tab switch, before closing a tab, and during app shutdown. Writes should be atomic: write to .tmp then replace/rename.

Startup recovery

On launch, if a session manifest exists, restore all open tabs, contents, order, active tab, cursor, scroll, dirty state, word wrap, and line number preference.

File-backed tabs

For clean file-backed tabs, it is acceptable to restore by reading from disk if the file exists and has not changed unexpectedly. Dirty file-backed tabs must restore the persisted unsaved content.

External file changes

If a disk-backed file changed externally while the app was closed, warn the user on restore before overwriting or saving.

Cleanup

When a tab is saved and closed or discarded and closed, remove its recovery blob. Keep current session state until the next successful manifest write.



Suggested manifest shape:

{
"version": 1,
"activeTabId": "tab-002",
"nextUntitledNumber": 4,
"tabs": [
{
"id": "tab-001",
"filePath": null,
"title": "Unfile-1",
"recoveryPath": "tabs/tab-001.txt",
"isDirty": true,
"syntax": "plainText",
"wordWrap": false,
"showLineNumbers": true,
"cursorLine": 12,
"cursorColumn": 8,
"verticalOffset": 0.0
}
]
}

6. Menus and Commands

Menu

Commands

File

New Tab, Open File, Save, Save As, Save All, Close Tab, Close All, Exit

Edit

Undo, Redo, Cut, Copy, Paste, Select All, Indent, Unindent

View

Toggle Word Wrap, Toggle Line Numbers, Toggle Markdown Preview, Theme selection if implemented

Search

Find, Find Next, Find Previous, Replace, Replace All

Format

Format JSON, Format XML

Settings

Editor preferences, default theme, recovery options if exposed

Help

About, Keyboard Shortcuts



7. Editor Behavior Details

Area

Details

Line numbers

Line number gutter must align with visible text lines. Toggle should be accessible from View menu and keyboard shortcut if implemented.

Word wrap

When enabled, long lines wrap visually without inserting newline characters. Search/replace and cursor movement must still operate on real document text.

Indentation

Use spaces only. Tab inserts 3 spaces or indents each selected line by 3 spaces. Shift+Tab removes up to 3 leading spaces from each selected line. Status bar should show Spaces: 3.

Auto indentation

New lines inherit leading whitespace from previous line. Increase indent after JSON/XML/YAML constructs where safe. Do not corrupt pasted text.

Syntax detection

Detect syntax from file extension on open/save. Unknown extensions default to plain text. User override is optional but recommended.

Formatting

JSON formatting should preserve valid data semantics. XML formatting should preserve valid XML semantics. On invalid content, show non-destructive error message and leave text unchanged.

Markdown preview

Preview uses split-panel: editor on left, rendered preview on right. Only show for Markdown content or when user manually toggles preview.

Search panel

Use an inline panel or modal-free bar. Highlight matches, display match count, and support keyboard-driven navigation.



8. Suggested Avalonia Architecture

  • Pattern: MVVM with one MainWindowViewModel containing ObservableCollection<EditorTabViewModel> and ActiveTab.

  • Views: MainWindow, EditorTabsView, EditorDocumentView, SearchReplacePanel, MarkdownPreviewView, and optional SettingsDialog.

  • Models: EditorDocument, EditorSessionManifest, RecoveryTabRecord, SyntaxLanguage enum, SaveResult enum.

  • Services: IFileDialogService, IFileSystemService, IRecoveryService, IFormattingService, ISyntaxService, IMarkdownPreviewService, ISearchReplaceService, IUserSettingsService.

  • Commanding: Expose ICommand/ReactiveCommand operations for menu items, keyboard shortcuts, toolbar/status actions, and tab close buttons.

Component

Responsibility

MainWindowViewModel

Owns tab collection, active tab, app-level commands, startup restore, shutdown persistence.

EditorTabViewModel

Owns document text, file path, display title, dirty state, syntax mode, cursor metadata, tab close command.

RecoveryService

Loads/saves manifest and content blobs atomically; cleans up closed tabs.

FormattingService

Pretty-prints JSON/XML and returns either formatted text or validation error.

SearchReplaceService

Searches active document text and performs replace operations with options.

MarkdownPreviewService

Converts Markdown text to rendered preview content.

SyntaxService

Maps file extensions to syntax modes and editor highlighting definitions.



9. Key Flows

Flow

Steps

Create new tab

User selects File > New Tab or plus button -> assign Unfile-N -> add tab -> activate tab -> persist manifest.

Open file

Show open dialog -> read file -> create tab with file name -> detect syntax -> mark clean -> persist manifest.

Edit content

Text change -> mark dirty -> update tab indicator -> debounce recovery write -> update Markdown preview if active.

Save file

If no path, run Save As -> write content to disk -> mark clean -> update title to file name -> update recovery state.

Close dirty tab

Prompt Save / Discard / Cancel -> Save writes file, Discard removes recovery blob, Cancel keeps tab open.

Restore session

On startup -> read manifest -> restore tab order/content/state -> activate previous tab -> show recovery status if dirty recovered content exists.



10. Keyboard Shortcuts

Shortcut

Action

Ctrl+T

New tab

Ctrl+O

Open file

Ctrl+S

Save active tab

Ctrl+Shift+S

Save all

Ctrl+W

Close active tab

Ctrl+Tab

Next tab

Ctrl+Shift+Tab

Previous tab

Ctrl+F

Find

Ctrl+H

Replace

F3 / Shift+F3

Find next / previous

Tab

Insert or apply 3-space indent

Shift+Tab

Apply 3-space unindent



11. Non-Functional Requirements

  • Runs on Windows and Linux with platform-appropriate window chrome and file dialogs.

  • Large files should remain usable; avoid blocking UI thread on file IO, formatting, Markdown preview, and recovery writes.

  • Recovery writes should be resilient to partial writes and interrupted processes.

  • No permanent file explorer/sidebar should appear by default or as part of the first release.

  • All destructive actions must be explicit and reversible where possible.

  • Use accessible labels for tab close buttons, menu commands, status bar fields, and search controls.

12. Edge Cases and Error Handling

Scenario

Expected behavior

Attempt to close dirty tab

Prompt Save / Discard / Cancel. Cancel leaves tab active and unchanged.

Save fails due to permission or locked file

Show error. Keep tab dirty and preserve recovery blob.

Formatting invalid JSON/XML

Show validation error with line/column when available. Do not alter document.

Recovery manifest corrupted

Attempt best-effort recovery from content blobs; show clear recovery message.

File deleted between sessions

Restore from recovery blob if available and show tab as dirty/recovered.

File renamed externally

Restore from recovery blob or warn that original path is missing.

Huge Markdown file preview

Throttle preview updates and avoid freezing editor input.

Search replace all with regex

Preview count before replace or make operation undoable as a single undo step.



13. Release Acceptance Checklist

  • Can open at least three files simultaneously and switch between them using tabs.

  • New documents are named Unfile-1, Unfile-2, and so on.

  • Disk files display only their file name in tabs.

  • Every tab shows an X close button.

  • Dirty tabs show an unsaved indicator and clean tabs do not.

  • Line numbers display correctly and can be toggled off/on.

  • Word wrap can be toggled off/on without modifying file contents.

  • Tab and Shift+Tab use exactly 3 spaces.

  • JSON and XML formatting commands work and fail safely.

  • YAML, JSON, and XML syntax highlighting is present.

  • Newline auto indentation works for common indentation patterns.

  • Markdown split preview can be toggled and updates from editor changes.

  • Search/replace supports find next/previous, replace current, replace all, match case, whole word, and regex.

  • After forced app termination, unsaved edits restore on next launch.

  • After normal app close and reopen, all open tabs restore with content, order, active tab, and dirty state.

14. Suggested Implementation Milestones

Milestone

Scope

M1: Shell and tabs

Avalonia app shell, menu bar, regular tabs, close buttons, new/open/save/close flows.

M2: Editor core

Line numbers, word wrap toggle, 3-space indentation, auto indentation, dirty state.

M3: Recovery

Session manifest, content blobs, startup restore, atomic writes, cleanup.

M4: Syntax and formatting

YAML/JSON/XML syntax modes and JSON/XML formatting menu commands.

M5: Markdown preview

Split-panel preview, throttled updates, preview toggle.

M6: Search/replace

Find/replace panel, options, highlighting, undo-safe replace operations.

M7: Polish and cross-platform QA

Windows/Linux visual QA, keyboard shortcuts, accessibility, large-file handling, error states.



Cross-Platform Engineer Text Editor Specification
